title: Access gate middleware — PAYMENT_ENABLED silent-fail, 402 on no active grant
acceptance_criteria: Exports 'createAccessGate(db: Pool): preHandlerHookHandler'. Returns a Fastify preHandler hook.
If PAYMENT_ENABLED is false (i.e. process.env.RAZORPAY_KEY_ID is absent or empty): hook calls done() immediately with no DB query — full pass-through.

If PAYMENT_ENABLED is true: queries access_grants for a row with status='active' AND (expires_at IS NULL OR expires_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '3 days'). If found: calls done(). If not found: calls done(new PaymentRequiredError()) which Fastify serialises to HTTP 402 with body {"error":"subscription_required","redirectTo":"/pricing"}.
The hook is registered on all /api/* routes EXCEPT /api/payment/* — this exclusion is enforced by the route prefix pattern, not by a blocklist inside the hook itself.
A custom error class 'PaymentRequiredError extends Error' is defined in this file with a statusCode = 402 property that Fastify's setErrorHandler reads.
The gate never queries DB more than once per request — no N+1 risk.
Clock used in the 3-day grace comparison is DB NOW() — not app-level Date.now() — so there is one authoritative clock source.

A comment explains the 3-day grace: 'Gives a paying user time to renew after expiry without immediately losing access. Grace period is inclusive: NOW() - 3 days means a subscription expired 2 days ago is still active.'
src/server.ts is modified only to register the access gate — no other changes.
output_format: src/payment/access-gate.ts plus minimal server.ts diff, plus plain-English note on why DB NOW() is used instead of app-side Date
